Member-only story

Web Development

Healthcare

Medical Devices

Healthcare Technology

Dicom

Building software for healthcare is very different from building a regular application. Here’s what changes when your users are doctors and your data belongs to patients.

7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Martha Dominguez de Gouveia on UnsplashIf you’re a web developer, you’ve probably built applications that look something like this:
A frontend.
A backend.
A database.
An authentication system.
Maybe a REST API.
Deploy everything to the cloud, add a CI/CD pipeline, and you’re ready to go.
But what happens when you decide to build something for healthcare?
Suddenly, things become a little more complicated.
Your application might be handling:

• Patient information

• Medical records

• Laboratory results

• Medical images

• Prescriptions

• Clinical notes
Now imagine that your application has a security vulnerability.
